data.title: JPMorgan Chase to pay Malaysia $330M to settle 1MDB case
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) agreed to pay Malaysia 1.4B ringgit ($330M) to settle all claims relating to 1MDB, the sovereign wealth fund that was the focus of one of the world's largest financial frauds, the government of Malaysia said Friday.

The settlement will end the U.S. lender's exposure to the scandal that came to light in 2015. In 2020, Goldman Sachs (GS) reached a $3.9B settlement with Malaysia to resolve claims that it misled investors in bond sales that raised $6.5B for the fund. The U.S. Department of Justice has determined that $4.5B was misappropriated from 1MDB. 

Also on Friday, Switzerland's Attorney General fined the JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) CHF 3M ($3.7M) for "failing to take all reasonable and necessary organizational measures" to prevent money laundering of the funds misappropriated from 1MDB.

In 2021, 1MDB sued units of JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM), Deutsche Bank (DB), and Coutts & Co., alleging "negligence, breach of contract, conspiracy to defraud/injure, and/or dishonest assistance," and sought to recover alleged losses.

Under the Malaysian agreement, JPMorgan (JPM) will, "without admission of liability,"  pay the amount to Malaysia's Assets Recovery Trust Account.

A JPMorgan spokesman commented, "We appreciate the collaboration with the Malaysian Government in resolving past matters related to 1MDB, which have been thoroughly reviewed. Since then, we’ve enhanced our controls, earning the trust of regulators in Switzerland and beyond. We are pleased to have these matters resolved and remain focused on delivering exceptional service to our clients."

Billions of dollars were funneled out of 1MDB starting in 2009, until the scandal was reported in 2015.

data.title: MCF Energy launches $500,000 private placement
data.description:
• MCF Energy (TSXV:MCF:CA) has announced a non-brokered private placement for aggregate minimum gross proceeds of $500,000.
  • The offering will consist of a minimum of 10M units at a price of $0.05 per unit.  Each unit will consist of one common share and one full common share purchase warrant, with each warrant entitling the holder to acquire an additional common share at an exercise price of $0.06 for a period of 24 months from the date of issuance.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Air Liquide to buy South Korea's DIG Airgas in $3.3B deal
data.description:
Air Liquide (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY) said Friday it agreed to acquire South Korea's DIG Airgas from Macquarie Asia-Pacific Infrastructure Fund 2 for €2.85B (~$3.3B) including debt.

The French company said the acquisition would strengthen its position in South Korea, the world’s fourth largest industrial gas market and sixth largest manufacturing country by GDP.

DIG reported revenues of €510M in 2024, has nearly 550 employees and 60 plants in South Korea, with operations in all major industrial areas, according to Air Liquide (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY).

The company said the acquisition represents a multiple of 20.2x EBITDA but falls to 14.8x when DIG's backlog of signed projects and expected cost synergies are taken into account, and decreases even more "when considering other growth synergies that will be generated by this very complementary acquisition."

The deal's indicated multiple was high relative to other valuations in the industry, but it still represents a "minor strategic positive" for Air Liquide (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY) as M&A opportunities in the highly consolidated industrial gases sector are limited, Jefferies analysts said in a note.

The deal would be Air Liquide's (OTCPK:AIQUF) (OTCPK:AIQUY) largest acquisition since the $13B purchase of Airgas a decade ago.

data.title: Cineplex renews share buyback program
data.description:
• Cineplex (TSX:CGX:CA) has received TSX approval to renew its normal course issuer bid (NCIB).
  • The company may repurchase up to 6.29M common shares, representing 10% of its public float.
  • As of August 14, 2025, Cineplex had 63.44M total shares outstanding.
  • Cineplex may begin purchases on or around August 26, 2025, with the program ending August 25, 2026.
  • Shares bought under the NCIB will be cancelled after purchase.
  • The buyback will be funded using Cineplex’s available cash.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: DeFi Technologies to launch normal course issuer bid
data.description:
Financial technology company DeFi Technologies (NASDAQ:DEFT) announced on Friday an intent to commence a normal course issuer bid.

Shares were +3.81% pre-market to $2.18.

The company plans to buy back shares of common stock with cash on hand through the facilities of Nasdaq Capital Markets, CBOE Canada, and/or other Canadian alternative trading platforms.

The current cash balance is ~$19.8M.

The buyback program will begin on August 26, 2025, and is expected to run through August 26, 2026.

Purchase price shall be the prevailing market price, and all shares repurchased will be canceled.

The actual number of shares purchased and the exact timing of such purchases will be determined by the company.

DeFi Technologies may not purchase any number of shares exceeding 10% of the public float as of August 18, or 31.67M shares, in aggregate.

data.title: European indexes cautious as markets digest EU-U.S. trade deal
data.description:
Update: The revised post reflects mrket movements as of 7.20am ET.

London (UKX) +0.05% to 9,314 following a record close above 9,300 the previous day.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.07% to 24,309. Germany's GDP expands 0.2% Y/Y in Q2

France (CAC:IND) +0.19% to 7,954. France’s business climate indicator held steady at 96 in August. France’s manufacturing climate indicator stayed at 96 in August.

Industrial inventories in Sweden rose by SEK1.96B in Q2.

Slovenia’s unemployment rate edged down to 4.3% in June.

Sweden’s unemployment rate rose to 8% in July.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) turned 0.04% higher to 559.3 on Friday, as investors turned cautious ahead of Fed Chair Jerome Powell’s Jackson Hole speech and weighed the impact of the EU’s new trade deal with the U.S. EU pledged $750 billion in U.S. energy spending and at least $600 billion in investment, securing a tariff cap of 15% instead of the threatened 30%. Pharmaceuticals will face 15% levy.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down less than 1 basis point to 4.33%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down less than 1 basis point to 2.75%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 4.75%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Restaurant rivals take on Starbucks during Pumpkin Spice Latte season
data.description:
Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) will bring back the Pumpkin Spice Latte on August 26. The date marks the return of the coffee chain's full fall menu, which will also include seasonal favorites like the Pumpkin Cream Cold Brew, Iced Pumpkin Cream Chai, and Pecan Crunch Oatmilk Latte. At-home PSL-inspired products are already available in grocery stores. The PSL release comes just before the start of the fall season and continues the tradition of introducing the phenomenon in late August each year.

As usual, restaurant rivals are not standing down in battling Starbucks (NASDAQ:SBUX) during pumpkin drink season.

McDonald's (NYSE:MCD) plans to bring McCafé Pumpkin Spice Latte (hot or iced) back on August 29.

Dunkin' is bringing Pumpkin Spice Latte (hot and iced), Pumpkin Cake Donuts and Pumpkin Munchkins Donut Holes, and Iced Pumpkin Loaf (pumpkin and vanilla cake with cream cheese icing) back in late August.

Dutch Bros. (NYSE:BROS) said it is channeling flannel this fall with the return of Caramel Pumpkin Brûlée and Cookie Butter Latte. The fall flavors are available starting August 22 at all +1,000+ Dutch Bros (NYSE:BROS) locations, while supplies last.

In addition, Tim Hortons (NYSE:QSR) relaunched its pumpkin spice beverage lineup, including Pumpkin Spice Latte, Pumpkin Spice Iced Capp, and Pumpkin Spice Cold Brew. Some locations are promoting a giveaway with every pumpkin drink purchase.

data.title: Biohaven rises as FDA cancels AdCom on lead drug
data.description:
Biohaven (NYSE:BHVN) shares spiked in the premarket on Friday after the FDA revoked an advisory committee meeting scheduled in connection with its marketing application for lead drug troriluzole.

Early this year, the New Haven, Connecticut-based biotech received the FDA’s priority review for its new drug application (NDA) for troriluzole targeted at spinocerebellar ataxia, a group of rare neurodegenerative disorders.

In May, the FDA’s Office of Neuroscience extended its review period to evaluate additional submissions made by the company in response to the agency’s requests and decided to hold an advisory committee meeting to discuss the NDA.

In an SEC filing on Friday, the company noted that the FDA informed it on Aug. 21 that an AdCom meeting is no longer required for the review.

Biohaven (NYSE:BHVN), a spinout of Biohaven Pharmaceutical formed when Pfizer (PFE) acquired the migraine therapy maker in 2022, continues to expect a decision on the NDA in Q4 2025.

data.title: Portman Ridge's name change to BCP Investment effective after market close today
data.description:
Investment company Portman Ridge Finance (NASDAQ:PTMN) said in an SEC filing on Thursday that its name change to BCP Investment is effective after market close on Friday, August 22.

On August 25, the company's shares of common stock will cease to trade under the ticker PTMN, and commence trading as BCIC.

PTMN had disclosed the name and ticker change plans, connected to its merger with Logan Ridge Finance (LRFC), on June 17.

data.title: BJ's Wholesale Club says it is on solid footing for the back half of the year
data.description:
BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings (NYSE:BJ) traded slightly lower in early action on Friday after the warehouse club chain posted a mixed Q2 earnings report.

Total comparable club sales decreased by 0.3% during the quarter due to declining retail prices of fuel. Excluding the impact of gasoline sales, comparable club sales increased by 2.3%. Top-line revenue of $5.38 billion fell short of the consensus estimate by $100 million.

Membership fee income increased 9.0% year-over-year to $123.3 million in Q2. The increase was primarily driven by strength in membership acquisition, retention, and higher-tier membership penetration across both new and existing clubs, as well as the increase in annual membership fees, which became effective in January 2025.

Merchandise gross margin rate increased by 20 basis points compared to the first six months of fiscal 2024. The company noted that it continues to manage the business to drive profitable growth across the broader merchandise assortment. Operating income was up 6.3% to $216.5 million. Non-GAAP EPS was reported at $1.14 vs. $1.09 consensus and $1.09 a year ago. Adjusted EBITDA rose 8.0% to $303.9 million.

"Our business model continues to perform and build upon momentum, as we grow membership and gain market share even in a dynamic environment. We enter the back half of the year on solid footing and confident in our ability to deliver strong results," stated CEO Bob Eddy.

Looking ahead, BJ's (NYSE:BJ) sees comparable club sales, excluding the impact of gasoline sales, increasing 2.0% to 3.5% year-over-year for the full year. Adjusted EPS is seen landing in a range from $4.20 to $4.35 (midpoint $4.275) vs. $4.30 consensus.

Shares of BJ's (BJ) slipped 0.5% in premarket trading to $106.00 vs. the 52-week range of $76.33 to $121.10. The warehouse club stock is still up more than 18% on a year-to-date basis.
